definitions: To tease, to play a trick on someone.
To make a loud, harsh noise.
To break or tear something into rags.
To engage in boisterous, often destructive, play or pranks (especially in a university context).
examples: They like to rag him about his new haircut.
The teacher ragged the students for their poor behavior.
The old engine began to rag and sputter.
He ragged the cloth until it was in shreds.
The students ragged the dorm during the celebration.
